title: Approximate pit-utility and rejoin-slot features
summary: Real pit calls depend on whether stopping now is actually advantageous given pit-loss, traffic gap, and undercut/overcut conditions, not just tyre wear.
rationale: Real pit calls depend on whether stopping now is actually advantageous given pit-loss, traffic gap, and undercut/overcut conditions, not just tyre wear. F1 strategy analysis repeatedly highlights clean-air rejoin windows, tyre warm-up asymmetry, and track-specific undercut/overcut balance.
implementation_hint: Build simple context features such as estimated pit-loss affordability by race, whether current position is inside an approximate rejoin gap, relative pace vs race-lap median, and whether current conditions favor undercut or overcut (`positive LapTime_Delta` on worn tyre vs low-energy tracks where fresh tyres may warm slowly). Use coarse race-level constants learned from train OOF summaries rather than hardcoded external values.
expected_effect: Adds decision-context signal missing from pure tyre-state features, especially around the ambiguous pit window where several cars have similar wear.
risk: Without explicit field gaps this must be approximate, and poorly estimated race constants can add noise. It may help public LB more than local CV if race mixes differ.
